summary: iter55.33 — Full backend + admin UI validation of the new user-admin RBAC gating. Backend: 10/10 test_iter55_33_user_admin_gating cases pass (catalog exposes user_stats/user_functions/view_user_sensitive; list endpoint strips phone/vip_balances/vip_balance_usdt/allowed_permissions/allowed_currencies/market_perms for staff without view_user_sensitive but keeps email/role/user_id; admin never filtered; empty allowed_permissions=[] preserves full legacy access; stats endpoint gated by user_stats returning 403 with Spanish 'permiso' wording; new stats fields kyc/success_rate_pct/favorite_currency/email_verified/phone_verified/twofa_enabled all present when authorized; PUT gated by user_functions when payload touches role/allowed_currencies/allowed_permissions/market_perms/account_status → 403 with 'Funciones de usuario' string; verify-email flow independent of user_functions gate). Adjacent regression: test_iter55_16_permissions had ONE outdated hardcoded len==13 assertion — updated to 16 (net +3 codes). Total pytest sweep after fix: 51/51 green across iter55.16/30/32/33. Frontend (viewport 1440x900, admin session): (a) /admin/users has EXACTLY the 6 required columns Usuario/Email/Rol/Saldo (USDT eq.)/Registrado/Acciones — legacy inline columns removed. (b) NO horizontal scroll (tableWidth 1138 == parentWidth 1138). (c) 40 user-stats-btn-* and 44 user-perms-btn-* rendered; buttons are large with label+icon (violet ESTADÍSTICAS, emerald FUNCIONES). (d) user-functions-dialog opens with tabs role/currencies/market/phone; perms tab correctly ABSENT for normal/vip targets and present only when target.role=employee. (e) AdminUserStatsPage renders admin-user-stats-page + user-stats-personal (email/phone/2FA/rol) + user-stats-kyc + kpi-success-rate + kpi-favorite-currency + net-position + back-btn. Frontend gating live-verified with a provisioned gated staff (allowed_permissions=['users']): (i) 45 balance-restricted-{uid} chips shown in list ('Restringido' label) instead of vip_balance_usdt values; (ii) direct GET on /admin/users/user_test_vip01/stats produced backend 403, frontend surfaced toast "No tienes el permiso 'Estadísticas de usuario' asignado. Contacta al admin para que te lo habilite." and redirected back to /admin/users; (iii) Funciones button is VISIBLE for gated staff (44/45 rows) matching the operator requirement; the account_status PUT on a target user returned an error toast although the localized 'Funciones de usuario' 403 detail did not display (see minor issue below).

integration_issues.issue: When a gated staff (['users'] only) opens the Funciones dialog and changes account_status via uf-status-select, the resulting 403 from PUT /api/admin/users/{id} appears in Network but the visible toast says the generic 'Error al guardar.' instead of the backend's Spanish detail 'Acceso restringido. Necesitas el permiso Funciones de usuario ...'. Code inspection (UserFunctionsDialog.jsx L76-82) expects `e.response?.status === 403` + string detail to surface the message, so this can happen if axios is unwrapping status differently or the DialogSelect swallows the promise rejection. NOT a security bug (backend rejects correctly, DB is untouched) — pure UX degradation.

action_items: Optional UX polish: ensure UserFunctionsDialog surfaces the backend 403 detail verbatim ('Acceso restringido. Necesitas el permiso Funciones de usuario …') instead of the generic 'Error al guardar.' fallback. Likely fix — inspect the CurrencyMultiSelect/onValueChange path and confirm `putUserField` awaits and error.response is populated (the same code path DOES surface it correctly for the stats page). Not blocking; backend gate is authoritative.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_users.py list endpoint: the sensitive-field stripping runs AFTER `_enrich_user_with_usdt_total` — meaning we still spend rate-lookup + math cycles for staff who won't see the number. Micro-optimisation: check the permission before enrichment (skip for gated staff). Not a functional bug.

AdminUsers.jsx: The role and currency edit toggles inside UserFunctionsDialog silently disable themselves when the viewer is a non-admin editing an admin/employee target. When a gated staff opens the dialog on THEIR OWN row (their user_id is in the list), the Rol select is disabled — expected — but no explanatory helper text is shown. Consider a small chip like 'Solo un admin puede modificar este rol' below the disabled select for parity with the perms tab.

UserFunctionsDialog.jsx putUserField catches 403 correctly, but the toast fallback string ('Error al guardar.') should probably read the response.data.detail when it exists even for non-403 errors (currently only stringified when top-level string). Consider unifying error-detail extraction into a helper.

The three new permission codes are correctly added to PERMISSION_CATALOG in stable order. `sanitize_permissions` will accept them automatically since it filters against VALID_CODES — no change needed there.

test_iter55_16_permissions.py had a brittle hardcoded len==13 assertion (updated to 16). Consider replacing exact-length asserts with subset asserts (`expected_codes.issubset(codes)`) so future additive-only iterations don't require test edits.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter55_16_permissions.py (updated hardcoded catalog length 13 → 16 and expanded expected code-set with user_stats/user_functions/view_user_sensitive to reflect iter55.33 additions)
test_credentials: ADMIN=test_session_admin_X. TOTP secret JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXPJBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP. For frontend gating tests a temporary staff session cookie 'test_session_staff_gated_ui_X' was provisioned via direct Mongo insert (allowed_permissions=['users']) and CLEANED UP at the end of the run — no residue in DB.
seed_data_creation: Temporary gated staff (user_id=user_test_staff_gated_ui, email=gated.staff.ui@resilience.com) provisioned during frontend gating validation and DELETED afterwards. No permanent seeds added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend gate hierarchy: `users` grants list access, `view_user_sensitive` reveals phone/balance/perm columns, `user_stats` grants /users/{id}/stats, `user_functions` grants PUT /users/{id} for role/currencies/perms/market/status. Legacy employees with allowed_permissions=[] still pass everything (verified by test_empty_permissions_grants_full_access_backward_compat). Backend 403 body is {detail: 'Acceso restringido. Necesitas el permiso ...'} as a plain string, NOT a dict — the frontend TotpPromptDialog's handleTotpError only intercepts when detail.code === 'TOTP_CODE_REQUIRED', so pure permission 403s should fall through to the error toast. AdminUserStatsPage handles this cleanly (toast + navigate back). UserFunctionsDialog handles it in code but the visible toast during live test showed the generic fallback — worth a second look. UserFunctionsDialog's role select is intentionally disabled when a non-admin views an admin/employee target (existing role-hardening from iter21, NOT the new iter55.33 gate). Data-testids in play for future E2E: user-stats-btn-{uid}, user-perms-btn-{uid}, user-functions-dialog, user-functions-tab-{role|currencies|perms|market|phone}, uf-role-select, uf-status-select, uf-{role|currencies|perms|market|phone}-tab, admin-user-stats-page, user-stats-personal, user-stats-kyc, user-stats-kpi-success-rate, user-stats-kpi-favorite-currency, user-stats-net-position, balance-restricted-{uid}.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature iteration completed cleanly. Only defect surfaced was an outdated hardcoded assertion in an older test file (fixed inline).
